channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Real-world comparative outcomes of darolutamide- versus abiraterone-based triplet therapy for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer: a retrospective cohort study
item.description: This retrospective cohort study compared real-world clinical outcomes between darolutamide- and abiraterone-based triplet therapy regimens in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer. The analysis evaluated the effectiveness and safety of these two androgen receptor inhibitor appro

item.title: STAT+: I spoke to Anthropic’s CEO about how AI may affect biotech. Here’s what I learned
item.description: Call AI in biotech hype at your peril. There are real reasons that pharmaceutical companies are embracing this technology right now.

item.title: STAT+: GOP lawmakers push Trump’s FDA to make sure clinical trials are diverse
item.description: Supporters say clinical trial diversity is about making sure drugs and devices work, not about DEI.

item.title: Novartis buys UK biotech myricx for up to $1.5bn to chase a new cancer-drug class
item.description: Novartis has agreed to buy Myricx Bio, a small British oncology company, for up to $1.5bn, adding an early-stage but potentially significant technology to a cancer pipeline the industry keeps racing to expand. The Swiss group will pay $1.1bn upfront, with up …

item.title: “abolish the nih”? dr. scott atlas gives the antiscience game away
item.description: Dr. Scott Atlas, a senior fellow at the right wing think tank Hoover Institution who came to prominence as a COVID contrarian, recently wrote an op-ed advocating the elimination of the National Institutes of health and leaving the funding biomedical research …

item.title: Using human 3d organoid models to gain mechanistic insight in motor neuron diseases
item.description: Motor neuron disorders involve complex interactions between multiple CNS cell types, which are challenging to model in 2D in vitro conditions. Frizzi and colleagues describe recent advances in induced pluripotent stem cell-based 3D (organoid) models of the sp…

item.title: Papastergiou: greece sets out AI rules under EU framework
item.description: Greece will set out its legal and institutional framework for the operation and applications of artificial intelligence this week through draft legislation submitted to parliament, Digital Governance Minister Dimitris Papastergiou… Papastergiou: Greece sets o…

item.title: Genetic analysis of imaging-derived phenotypes
item.description: When integrated with genetic data, imaging-derived phenotypes (IDPs) enable large-scale analyses of genotype–phenotype relationships. Bian and Akey review analytical approaches to derive IDPs, key biological insights from genetic studies of imaging traits and…

item.title: Meta’s woes deepen in india as child abuse ads on instagram draw government ire
item.description: U.S. social media giant Meta is facing an onslaught of regulatory scrutiny in India, which has the largest user base for Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp.

item.title: Tracking AI in the supply chain
item.description: Supply chains have become a key testing ground for AI as businesses manage vast data volumes and complex networks

item.title: Fibronectin-induced overactivation of αvβ3-pi3k-pip3-pdk1-ilk signaling drives aortic disease in marfan syndrome
item.description: Here, the authors identify a fibronectin-driven signaling pathway linking extracellular matrix changes to aortic disease in Marfan syndrome. Targeting this pathway prevents and reverses aortic damage in models, suggesting new therapeutic strategies.

item.title: Antibody-oligonucleotide conjugates: design principles, intracellular delivery, and translational opportunities
item.description: This review examines antibody-oligonucleotide conjugates, focusing on their design principles, mechanisms for achieving intracellular delivery, and potential clinical applications. The work addresses key considerations for developing these hybrid therapeutics that combine the targeting specificity o

item.title: FLOWR.ROOT - A flow matching-based foundation model for joint multi-purpose structure-aware 3D ligand generation and affinity prediction
item.description: FLOWR.ROOT is a flow matching-based foundation model that enables joint multi-purpose 3D ligand generation and binding affinity prediction while incorporating structural information about target proteins. The model, developed by researchers at Pfizer's Machine Learning & Computational Sciences and C

item.title: STAT+: Four major biotech updates to catch up on
item.description: Anthropic CEO talks drug discovery, Medicare proposes steep 340B drug payment cuts, and more biotech news

item.title: Secret Claude tracker shocks users after Anthropic’s anti-surveillance stance
item.description: Anthropic accused of spying on users; engineer says “experiment” is over.

item.title: White house adviser says trump won’t create ‘fda for ai’
item.description: The White House’s outgoing artificial intelligence adviser says the administration won’t require formal licensing for the technology. “There will not be an FDA for AI,” Sriram Krishnan said in an interview with the Financial Times (FT) Friday (July 3), in ref…

item.title: Ημερήσια γεωπολιτική επισκόπηση - 05/07/2026
item.description: Edited by iEpikaira* [Σημ.: Η ανάλυση παρατίθεται παρακάτω και στα ελληνικά.] Executive Summary The international strategic environment continues to be shaped by mounting systemic pressures across the energy, economic and security domains. Recent d…

item.title: Canada’s talent hunt: why the country needs more skilled workers than ever
item.description: If successful with its science and engineering focused immigration strategy, Canada could strengthen both its innovation economy and its long-term economic resilience. The post Canada’s talent hunt: Why the country needs more skilled workers than ever appeare…

item.title: Non-surgical procedure halves knee pain over 12-month trial
item.description: Safe, simple, effective.ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here.

item.title: Almost 90 new unicorns have been minted so far this year — here they are | techcrunch
item.description: Almost 90 new unicorns have been minted so far this year — here they are | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: Understanding the structure of swine leukocyte antigen class I molecules
item.description: This study examines the structural characteristics of swine leukocyte antigen (SLA) class I molecules, which are important for understanding immune recognition in swine. The research was conducted by investigators from Dalian University and affiliated institutions in China, with findings relevant to

item.title: Ciliation and mucus secretion in an in vitro model of nasal mucosa based on CI-pNaEC cell line for preclinical drug testing
item.description: Researchers developed an in vitro nasal mucosa model using the CI-pNaEC cell line to evaluate ciliation and mucus secretion for preclinical drug testing. This model system enables assessment of key functional characteristics of nasal tissue relevant to pharmaceutical development and safety evaluatio

item.title: Organoid-AI platforms need integrated governance in drug discovery
item.description: Organoid-AI platforms are functioning as integrated decision systems in drug discovery, influencing compound prioritization, toxicity assessment, and program progression. Current governance approaches validate the biological and computational components separately, despite their interdependent roles

item.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
item.description: Mistral AI, which offers some open source AI models, has raised significant funding since its creation in 2023, with the ambition to “put frontier AI in the hands of everyone.”

item.title: Japan looks to economic security as u.s. turns more transactional
item.description: Japan is seeking to turn economic security into diplomatic leverage as the United States deepens its America First approach, with policymakers focusing on semiconductors, critical minerals, energy, shipbuilding and advanced technology as areas where Japan can…

item.title: China is rewriting e-commerce law to tighten platform rules at home and shield its companies abroad
item.description: China released draft amendments to its e-commerce law on Saturday, proposing 20 provisions that would expand the law’s reach beyond platforms and merchants to cover a wider range of participants in the digital economy. The proposal, jointly issued by the Stat…

item.title: Beyond obesity: how glp-1s are changing the game for hundreds of diseases
item.description: Beyond obesity: How GLP-1s are changing the game for hundreds of diseasespharmaphorum.com

item.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using claude code | techcrunch
item.description: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: What is mistral AI? everything to know about the openai competitor | techcrunch
item.description: Mistral AI, which offers some open source AI models, has raised significant funding since its creation in 2023, with the ambition to “put frontier AI in the hands of everyone.”

item.title: Confidential computing's core trust mechanism is broken. the fix may not exist
item.description: Attested TLS: the handshake that can't prove who's on the other end

item.title: Oral microbiome modulation mitigates hyperglycemia exacerbation in gestational diabetes mellitus
item.description: Here, the authors associate changes in the oral microbiome during gestational diabetes with gum inflammation and blood sugar control, and show in animal models and a human trial that restoring healthier oral bacteria or topical docosahexaenoic acid, reduce th…

item.title: Daily current affairs ias | upsc prelims and mains exam – 29th june 2026
item.description: Archives (PRELIMS Focus) (MAINS Focus) The post DAILY CURRENT AFFAIRS IAS | UPSC Prelims and Mains Exam – 29th June 2026 appeared first on IASbaba.

item.title: The AI trade is losing one of its key signals
item.description: AI trade signals weaken as pricing power declines, raising concerns over future profitability and market sustainability.

item.title: The only AI glossary you'll need this year | techcrunch
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: Journalist karen hao on sam altman, openai & the "quasi-religious" push for artificial intelligence
item.description: As part of our July Fourth special broadcast, we continue our extended interview with Karen Hao, author of Empire of AI: Dreams and Nightmares in Sam Altman’s OpenAI. The book documents the rise of OpenAI and how the AI industry is leading to a new form of co…

item.title: Is AI a threat to healthcare jobs? which jobs are and aren’t at risk
item.description: Will AI replace healthcare jobs? Not exactly. Learn which roles face the greatest disruption, which remain resilient, and how the workforce is evolving.

item.title: Ibio expands cardiometabolic and obesity program with anti-myostatin antibody discovered using its proprietary platform, in-licensed from astralbio
item.description: iBio Expands Cardiometabolic and Obesity Program with Anti-Myostatin Antibody Discovered Using its Proprietary Platform, In-Licensed from AstralBioibioinc.com

item.title: Icas members call for bigger government role in AI regulation
item.description: An ICAS survey found that 60% of CAs want greater government intervention in AI regulation.

item.title: Openai offers feds a stake, anthropic gets out of AI model jail and meta wants to be a neocloud
item.description: OpenAI reportedly has floated giving the U.S. government a 5% stake in the company, perhaps the start of a series of such stakes in other AI companies as well. This no doubt has traditional anti-industrial-policy Republicans, all apparently dead now, spinning…

item.title: AI law may be considered as time is getting right, export curbs eased on mythos: meity secretary s krishnan
item.description: The Centre may begin work on a dedicated legal framework for artificial intelligence (AI) as the technology continues to evolve, Secretary, Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), S Krishnan, said on Friday, indicating that the government …

item.title: Cloud trust is becoming the next cybersecurity battleground for AI
item.description: Organisations increasingly need to prove that infrastructure, workloads and data environments remain trustworthy throughout their lifecycle. The post Cloud trust is becoming the next cybersecurity battleground for AI appeared first on Digital Journal.

item.title: FDA oral glp-1 vote extends obesity drug rally across biotech - bullishdaily
item.description: FDA support for an oral GLP-1 obesity drug strengthens the metabolic franchise trade, lifting obesity-focused biotech while intensifying large-cap pharma competition.

item.title: Frontiers | association between mental health service utilization and diabetes-hypertension comorbidity: a community-based study
item.description: ObjectiveTo examine the association between mental health service (MHS) utilization and diabetes–hypertension (DM–HTN) comorbidity and to explore the roles o...

item.title: Texas oil regulator directs doge to enhance rrc efficiency
item.description: 'Our goal is simple', RRC Commissioner Wayne Christian said.

item.title: The browser wars aren't about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to chrome and safari | techcrunch
item.description: The browser wars aren't about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
item.description: At the event "The Briefing: AI for Science" earlier this week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a new "AI workbench for scientists" that pulls fragmented tools and datasets into one environment, and generates figures and visuals. Anthropic, already dominat…

item.title: AI bills are baffling the c-suite after shift to usage-based pricing
item.description: KPMG finds nearly a third of execs struggle to understand costs as companies rethink deployments

item.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
item.description: Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic writer could be caught in the crossfire. Broad distaste around the u…

item.title: Review on drug repositioning of some FDA-approved drugs for treatment of cancer/microbial diseases
item.description: This review examines the repositioning of FDA-approved drugs for treatment of cancer and microbial diseases. Drug repositioning leverages pre-existing safety and pharmacokinetic data from approved medications, substantially reducing development timelines and costs compared to de novo drug developmen

item.title: STAT+: A ‘historic’ FDA clearance raises the question: Is LLM the interface? Or the decision-maker?
item.description: The newly cleared device is based around an app that helps a patient manage their diabetes using a treatment plan defined by their doctor.
